rag_rules: Repair latched expression misuse by ensuring it appears only in implementation scopes and targets valid input ports.
Resolve type mismatches by validating that all operands in binary expressions are of type bool when using logical operators.
Fix syntax errors like mismatched 'and' by ensuring parentheses surround boolean subexpressions and operators are correctly applied.
generic_rules: Only use identifiers visible in the current scope—either AADL features or locally declared eq helpers.
Ensure logical expressions use bool-typed operands and avoid implicit type coercion.
When AGREE reports unresolved types, trace to the first untyped or misdeclared element on the same line.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use 'and', 'or', 'not(...)' for boolean logic; never use '&&' or '||'.
Binary expressions like 'and' require fully parenthesized boolean operands.
eq helpers must declare types as int, real, or bool—never 'integer' or 'boolean'.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type annexes.
Avoid using untyped or implicitly declared identifiers in guarantees.
Do not attempt to repair type errors by adding new architecture—fix the root declaration or remove the clause.
